description_long: This is a vintage biplane with a wooden-textured fuselage and two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically. The upper and lower wings both feature roundels with blue, white, and red concentric circles, reminiscent of early 20th-century military aircraft. The nose houses a single wooden propeller, and the engine cowling is painted a light gray. The tail section includes a vertical stabilizer with blue and red stripes, and a horizontal stabilizer with a simple rectangular shape. The cockpit is open and situated just behind the upper wing, with a small windshield and visible seat. The landing gear consists of two main wheels and a small tail skid, all fixed and non-retractable.
description: A vintage wooden biplane with stacked wings, roundel markings, an open cockpit, and fixed landing gear.
description_view_0: Side view showing the stacked biplane wings, open cockpit, and tail with blue and red stripes.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the propeller, engine cowling, and the symmetrical arrangement of the wings.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the wing structure, cockpit, and tail design from the other profile.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the tail assembly, vertical stabilizer, and the open cockpit behind the wings.
attribute_wingspan_configuration: Biplane configuration with two straight, parallel wings stacked vertically; no winglets or pronounced dihedral/anhedral angles.
attribute_engine_type: Single front-mounted piston engine with a wooden propeller.
attribute_tail_design: Conventional tail with a single vertical stabilizer and horizontal stabilizers.
attribute_landing_gear_type: Taildragger configuration with two fixed main wheels and a fixed tail skid.
attribute_cockpit_placement: Open, single-seat cockpit located just behind the upper wing, forward-mounted on the fuselage.
